name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Chicago, Il
description_short: NACA is a non-profit housing advocacy organization providing HUD-approved housing counseling, character-based mortgages with no down payment or closing costs, and financial literacy services to underserved communities.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union as the first organization to establish a housing trust fund. Over 36 years, the organization has evolved into a comprehensive housing advocacy and lending platform focused on challenging predatory lending practices and expanding homeownership access to low- to moderate-income individuals and communities of color. The organization serves as both a non-profit counselor and a mortgage lender operating under a mission of economic justice.

NACA offers character-based mortgages featuring no down payment, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and below-market fixed rates (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026). The organization does not consider credit scores in lending decisions, instead evaluating applicants based on their circumstances and character. Members access services through Achieve the Dream Events held nationwide, where they can work with NACA staff to become NACA Qualified. The platform includes a member portal for document upload, status tracking, and information submission. NACA also provides HUD-approved housing counseling services and fights corporate landlord practices through advocacy campaigns.

NACA distinguishes itself through its explicit focus on closing racial wealth disparities and challenging industry predatory practices. The organization has helped 500,000 homeowners, provided 75,000 NACA mortgages, and made $20 billion in mortgage commitments. NACA represents 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally. The organization operates as both a lender and advocate, maintaining a volunteer network (NACtivist program) and accepting donations to support its mission. Their character-based lending model explicitly rejects traditional credit score evaluation.

The main limitation is that NACA mortgages require participation in in-person Achieve the Dream Events (multi-day weekend events in specific cities) rather than offering fully remote or walk-in counseling. Service availability is geographically limited to event locations. The organization's focus on advocacy and mission-driven lending may mean longer processing timelines compared to commercial lenders. Additionally, while no down payment is offered, applicants must still meet NACA's character-based qualification standards.
pros: No down payment required on mortgages
No closing costs, fees, or mortgage insurance
Credit score is not considered in lending decisions
Provides HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of all national HUD counseling)
Below-market fixed interest rates (5.625% 30-year as of April 2026)
Serves 3 million people with $20 billion in mortgage commitments
Non-profit mission explicitly focused on closing racial wealth disparities
cons: Requires participation in multi-day in-person Achieve the Dream Events to qualify (not available online)
Limited geographic availability tied to scheduled event locations
Long historical processing times due to character-based underwriting and non-profit structure
Mortgage rates and availability subject to membership qualification standards beyond traditional credit metrics
best_for: Low to moderate-income homebuyers with limited savings for down payments
First-time homebuyers from historically underserved communities and people of color
Individuals with poor or non-existent credit seeking character-based lending alternatives
Buyers in or near NACA event cities (Hartford CT, Little Rock AR, Milwaukee WI, Fort Myers FL) seeking affordable homeownership
diagnosis: NACA is best for income-qualified first-time homebuyers, particularly people of color and those with poor credit, who can attend in-person events and qualify through character-based evaluation. The critical caveat is that NACA requires multi-day event participation in specific cities—this is not a remote or walk-in service and availability depends on scheduled Achieve the Dream Events.
services: Character-based mortgage lending with no down payment
Member portal for document upload and status tracking
Achieve the Dream multi-day in-person qualification events
Mortgage rate locking and commitment services
Predatory landlord advocacy and complaint documentation
Financial support and donations for mission advancement
Vendor/partner portals for lenders, servicers, and real estate professionals
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
